headline: The Quiet Privatization of Public Hospitals
context: MOH awarded 14 new hospital support service contracts to private firms in 2025 while public health allocation as a share of GDP fell to 2.2%, below the WHO threshold.

cards.big: Public hospitals. Private operators. Nobody announced the shift.
cards.sub: Outsourcing expanded to pathology labs and specialist clinics in seven states.

cards.big: Health spending: 2.2% of GDP. WHO minimum: 5%.
cards.sub: Malaysia has spent below the threshold every year since independence.

cards.big: Fourteen new privatization contracts. Zero tabled in Parliament.
cards.sub: MOH used direct negotiation, not open tender, citing 'operational urgency.'

cards.big: B40 patients wait 137 days for specialist appointments.
cards.sub: Private alternative: 3 days. The system creates its own demand for privatization.

cards.big: Is this policy or self-fulfilling prophecy?
cards.sub: Underfund public healthcare long enough and privatization becomes the only answer.

cards.big: The decision to privatize was made through two decades of underfunding, not through any single contract.
